articles.content: Wintersteiger V‑Edge: Versatile Tuning for Every Skier Provides New Revenue Opportunities for Ski Industry Retailers Salt Lake City, UT (February 26, 2026) — Wintersteiger has renewed its emphasis on V-Edge, the variable‑edge grinding function built into all Jupiter automated ski service machines, providing retailers with high‑margin services that improve turn and exit initiation for skiers of all disciplines. “V-Edge gives shops a simple, reliable way to deliver a noticeable customer benefit and drive incremental revenue from the equipment they already have,” said Wes Price, Sales Manager at Wintersteiger. “It’s easy to train staff on the Comfort, Sport, and Race settings, and the consistent results help shops convert more rentals, increase repeat business, and strengthen guest loyalty.” Key benefits for ski shops: Jupiter owners can offer V-Edge tuning as an upsell for rental fleets, demo skis, retail, and race customers, without additional major hardware purchases. Three pre-program

articles.title: Pila Records 12,000 Skiers in One Day as Season Surges
articles.description: Pila ski resort records 12,000 visitors in one day during Carnival weekend, with season entries up 9% and revenue up 16% as weather disruptions affect neighbouring resorts. The post Pila Records 12,000 Skiers in One Day as Season Surges appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: Pila Records a Historic Ski Day The ski resort of Pila in the Aosta Valley has recorded a historic peak of 12,000 skier visits in a single day over the Carnival weekend, confirming what is shaping up to be one of the strongest winter seasons in recent years. Early morning queues formed at the Aosta–Pila gondola base station as visitors arrived in large numbers. Despite the crowds, slope conditions remained good throughout the day thanks to solid snow cover and efficient lift operations. Planning a trip to Pila? For detailed piste maps, where to stay, lift access tips and insider advice, read my full Must-Read Guide to Pila Ski Resort . Season Performance: Growth Across the Board With roughly two months remaining before the scheduled May 3 closure, Pila’s winter 2025/26 figures are already ahead of last year: +9% increase in first entries +16% increase in revenue More than 400,000 skier entries recorded so far Over €14 million in turnover The numbers confirm a season that is not only bu

articles.title: French Ski Resorts Invest €555 Million in 2025, Maintaining Record-Level Commitment
articles.description: French ski resorts invested €555 million in 2025, maintaining a historically high level of spending and reinforcing the sector’s central role in mountain economies, according to the annual investment survey conducted by Montagne Leaders magazine in partnership with Atout France and Domaines Skiables de France. The total is nearly identical to 2024 and stands 50% above the ten-year average, keeping […] The post French Ski Resorts Invest €555 Million in 2025, Maintaining Record-Level Commitment ap
articles.content: Ski resorts across France including Tignes (pictured) received €555 million worth of investments in 2025. | Image: SnowBrains French ski resorts invested €555 million in 2025, maintaining a historically high level of spending and reinforcing the sector’s central role in mountain economies, according to the annual investment survey conducted by Montagne Leaders magazine in partnership with Atout France and Domaines Skiables de France. The total is nearly identical to 2024 and stands 50% above the ten-year average, keeping investment near record levels since the barometer was created. According to the survey, investment in 2025 represents 32% of pre-tax turnover—nine percentage points above the 10-year average—demonstrating how large a share of revenue operators are directing back into infrastructure and modernization. Related: Les Arcs, France, Report: Last Run of the Trip… 22 Powder Days in a Row Here! Industry representatives say sustaining that level of commitment has become more com

articles.title: French Ski Resorts Invest for the Future
articles.description: French ski resorts invested €555m in 2025, confirming the key role of investment in mountain regions. Much is at high altitude and main resorts see a bright future ahead. NEW The post French Ski Resorts Invest for the Future appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: In 2025, French ski resorts committed €555m (£486)in investments, according to the annual survey conducted by Montagne Leaders magazine in partnership with Atout France and Domaines Skiables de France. This amount is identical to the previous year and is 50% above the ten-year average. It keeps investment at a record level since the barometer was created. In terms of turnover, the investment represents 32% of pre-tax turnover, which is 9 points above the ten-year average, confirming a particularly high level of financial commitment. The breakdown of investments for 2025 by mountain range, excluding grooming, is €230.26m (£201.8m) in Savoie €84m (£73.5m) in Haute-Savoie €80.16m (£70.2m) in Isère €71.84m (£63m)in the Southern Alps €34.9m (£30.5m) in the Pyrenees, €0.88m (£0.77m)in the Vosges €1.17 m (£1.02m)in the Jura €5.61m (£4.9m)in the Massif Central Sustained effort in a context of increased constraints Long-term analysis shows that ski areas must devote an increasingly large share

articles.title: Mad River Glen, VT, Buys 1,100 Acres to Conserve Land for Backcountry Use
articles.description: Mad River Glen has finalized the purchase of more than 1,100 acres of land surrounding its ski area in Fayston, Vermont, securing the property for conservation and backcountry recreation. The cooperatively owned ski area closed the $2.5 million deal Tuesday, general manager Matt Lillard told VTDigger. The acquisition more than doubles the land owned by the resort’s cooperative, which previously […] The post Mad River Glen, VT, Buys 1,100 Acres to Conserve Land for Backcountry Use appeared first
articles.content: Mad River Glen has purchased a surrounding 1,100 acres of land for recreational and conservation purposes. Photo: Mad River Glen’s iconic single chair. Credit: Vermont.com Mad River Glen has finalized the purchase of more than 1,100 acres of land surrounding its ski area in Fayston, Vermont, securing the property for conservation and backcountry recreation. The cooperatively owned ski area closed the $2.5 million deal Tuesday, general manager Matt Lillard told VTDigger . The acquisition more than doubles the land owned by the resort’s cooperative, which previously held about 700 acres. Lillard said the property will not be developed and instead will be protected for recreational and conservation purposes. “We will be putting the land in conservation with a focus on recreation and backcountry skiing. We’re not looking to develop it in any way,” he told VTDigger . The purchase was financed through a crowdsourced fundraising effort that raised roughly $2.8 million, according to the resort

articles.title: Majority of Western United States with Record Low Snowpack: How it Affects Ski Resorts and What it Means For the Summer
articles.description: This winter has been historic in all the wrong ways for the West. October gave us hope with cold temperatures and early snowfall, but then it warmed up and dried out in November. December threw much of the region into monsoon season when the jet stream pushed warm South Pacific air and moisture into the Cascades and northern Rockies. Then […] The post Majority of Western United States with Record Low Snowpack: How it Affects Ski Resorts and What it Means For the Summer appeared first on SnowBrai
articles.content: Water content within snowpack is below average across the entire West. | Photo Credit: drought.gov This winter has been historic in all the wrong ways for the West. October gave us hope with cold temperatures and early snowfall, but then it warmed up and dried out in November. December threw much of the region into monsoon season when the jet stream pushed warm South Pacific air and moisture into the Cascades and northern Rockies. Then New Year’s came, and Mother Nature decided to take part in Dry January. Related: February 2026: State of the Western U.S. Snowpack—Trending in the Wrong Direction Images from the top of Arapahoe Basin show just how low the snowpack is, with plenty of exposed rock and dirt. | Photo Credit: Arapahoe Basin Ski Area/Facebook It’s currently the peak of winter, and some major ski destinations have less than two-thirds of their terrain open. Breckenridge is operating 115 of 193 runs, Snowbasin 72 of 115, and Park City a mere 214 of 349. Related: SnowBrains Fore

articles.title: Camelback Mountain Resort, PA, Surges with Record Growth, New Leadership, and Major Mountain Improvements
articles.description: As the 2025-26 ski season powers through winter storms and favorable conditions, Camelback Mountain Resort in Pennsylvania’s Poconos Mountains has emerged as a standout success story, boasting significant increases in skier visitations. Data from the popular ski tracking app Slopes, combined with insights from resort leadership, paint a picture of robust growth driven by strategic investments, an accessible location, and […] The post Camelback Mountain Resort, PA, Surges with Record Growth, New
articles.content: Camelback Resort is about 1 and a 1/2 hours from NYC and about 2 hours from Philadelphia. | Image: Camelback Mountain Resort As the 2025-26 ski season powers through winter storms and favorable conditions, Camelback Mountain Resort in Pennsylvania’s Poconos Mountains has emerged as a standout success story, boasting significant increases in skier visitations. Data from the popular ski tracking app Slopes , combined with insights from resort leadership, paint a picture of robust growth driven by strategic investments, an accessible location, and innovative marketing. Camelback saw a 126% increase in visitations during Christmas week 2025 compared to the same period in 2024, making it the fastest-growing ski resort in the country for that holiday stretch, according to data released by Slopes. This surge aligns with broader trends in the Northeast, where resorts experienced gains while Western U.S. destinations faced declines due to a lack of snow and poor conditions. Slopes’ location-bas

articles.title: Swiss Ski Resorts Are Changing
articles.description: Ski resorts and winter sports destinations are re-thinking what they offer. That’s according to a report on SwissInfo. NEW The post Swiss Ski Resorts Are Changing appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: The publication lists five main changes underway. See here for the full article on SwissInfo , and we summarise it below. Ski resorts are changing ownership Swiss ski resorts are mainly owned by cable car and lift companies, in which local communities often hold a stake but do not have majority control. In autumn 2025, the municipalities of Flims, Laax and Falera, in the canton of Graubünden, made headlines when they banded together to buy their ski area, “Weisse Arena”. Vail Resorts from the United States, the world’s largest ski resort operator, has set its sights on the Alps. It has already acquired the ski lifts of Crans-Montana and Andermatt-Sedrun. Vail Resorts Confirms it is looking to buy more resorts in the Alps Crans Montana – photo c/o Snow Camp The company often focuses on sites with development potential where lifts, accommodation and leisure facilities can be combined into one huge resort. The presence of these new investors is changing the character of the mountain regio

articles.title: How Arapahoe Basin Factored Into The U.S. Ski Industry’s Consolidation
articles.description: How Arapahoe Basin has factored into the multi-mountain pass battle between the Alterra Mountain Company and Vail Resorts.
articles.content: Colorado — While some skiers don’t like it, big ski economics are here to stay. The subscription-like model of multi-mountain passes has generated reliable revenue for major ski resort corporations, particularly Vail Resorts and the Alterra Mountain Company. It’s proven to be a vital source of income during low-snow years, such as the one we’re living through. It’s allowed skiers to basically go to any ski region for vacation, but it also has brought crowds. A relatively new video from Bloomberg Television dives into the mega-pass model and how it has propelled Vail and Alterra into household names among skiers. Bloomberg interviewed Rob Katz, CEO of Vail Resorts, and Jared Smith, the CEO of Alterra Mountain Company, to ask them how the industry has changed. Arapahoe Basin I’d argue the other main story the video tells is Arapahoe Basin’s role in the season pass wars. In the 1990s, Arapahoe Basin, Breckenridge, and Keystone were sold to Vail, when it was much smaller. However, the DOJ

articles.title: Conservation Groups Line Up Against Canadian Ski Resort’s Revival
articles.description: Conservation groups are speaking out against the conversion of a partially abandoned Alberta ski resort into a four-season destination.
articles.content: Alberta — The public comment period for the revival of Fortress Mountain Resort has come and gone. However, the debate over its proposed size is far from over. February 27th was the last day of the public comment period for growing Fortress Mountain Resort from its decrepit state into a four-season destination. The first phase would focus on summer business, with additions such as a few gondolas (including a scenic gondola to Whiskey Ridge), ziplining, a mountain coaster, a canyon swing, and hiking and biking trails. There would be some winter activities, such as cat-skiing and snowshoeing (which are currently available). Lift-served skiing is part of the second phase of development, which would feature all-new lifts. One challenge skiing operations face is heavy winds, which factored into the resort’s demise a few decades ago. Pushback Before the public comment period closed, various conservation groups spoke out against the plans. There are concerns that the public lands proposal is

articles.title: British Columbia Ski Resort Opens First Phase Of Large Terrain Expansion
articles.description: The first phase of the Pinegrove expansion opened this winter, which has added several new trails to a BC ski resort.
articles.content: Quesnel, British Columbia — After decades of dreaming and planning, along with a few years of clearing and construction, a new terrain expansion has opened at an under-the-radar ski resort in British Columbia. Back in January, Troll Resort opened its first runs as part of the Pinegrove expansion. This new terrain pod is serviced by a t-bar, which is one of the longest on the continent. The Black T-Bar has a 100-meter 49.4% grade near the beginning, but riders barely notice the steepness thanks to the detailed layout and engineering. The terrain pod is full of steep terrain, but also features meandering trails for those who are less experienced. This winter has seen the opening of four new trails in this terrain pod. The first two trails opened up in January: Lars’ Legacy (black) and Longest Way Down (green). After an extended closure due to a low snowpack, they reopened these trails this past weekend. They also opened two more trails, North Face and Best Day Ever, both black diamonds.

articles.title: Small British Columbia Ski Resort Having Turbulent Season
articles.description: After some high-level employees left the ski resort this past fall, they have struggled with operations this winter.
articles.content: British Columbia — It’s been a topsy-turvy winter for Fairmont Hot Springs Resort . Before the season started, there were reportedly several notable staff departures, raising questions about whether it would operate at all this winter. However, it is operating, albeit with some modifications compared to the typical season. According to The Columbia Valley Pioneer , ski season began for Fairmont on January 9th, which was a couple of weeks later than usual. However, it opened with limited lifts and terrain. This was due to the chairlift failing inspection twice. Add in warmer-than-average temperatures and inconsistent snowfall, and it’s been frustrating among guests. The chairlift finally opened on February 17th. Some guests were thrilled, while others said they wouldn’t renew their passes next year because of the delay. “It’s great that the chair is open and the recent snow will help extend the season a bit, but for many the damage is done. The poor — or non existent — communication to

Europe: Les 7 Laux, France Bergbahnen Hohsaas AG, Switzerland Valmorel, France Stöten i Sälen, Sweden Levi, Finland Stranda Ski Resort, Norway North America: Murray Ski Ridge, Canada Pebble Creek Ski Area, USA Thrill Hills, USA Bethel Village Trails XC, USA Franconia Inn & Outdoor Center XC, USA Great Glen Trails Outdoor Center XC, USA Northern Maine Community Trails XC, USA Steamboat Ski Touring Center XC, USA Haymaker Nordic Center XC, USA The Loppet XC, USA The Indy Pass will continue to add resorts over the off-season and expects major additions before the 26/27 season. “Indy Pass has experienced remarkable growth and with that growth comes responsibility,” said Indy Pass Director, Erik Mogensen. “We must continue

articles.title: It’s Looking Good For Late Season Skiing in the Alps
articles.description: It's set to be a great end to the winter ski season in the Alps. Favourable holiday dates, plenty of snow and then there's the Olympic affect. PlanetSKI looks ahead to skiing into Spring. NEW The post It’s Looking Good For Late Season Skiing in the Alps appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: UK operators report that Easter bookings are busy at the moment with resorts also reporting growing interest and bookings. This increased interest in Easter is reflected in a significant increase in online search traffic according to Google, which reports a rise of a double digit increase across Easter ski holiday searches. This year Easter Sunday is April 5th – last year it was two weeks later on April 20th. The second half of February saw snow across the Alps and some very significant falls in places. We reported on the snow at the time on PlanetSKI: The half-term PlanetSKI snow report Images across social media sparked interest. Les Menuires, France. Image c/o Les Menuires Tourist Office. Les Menuires, France. Image c/o Les Menuires Tourist Office. articles.title: Two Ski Mountaineers Found Dead After Avalanche on Tamlspitze, Tyrol
articles.description: Two ski mountaineers, aged 44 and 47, were found dead after an avalanche on Tamlspitze in Tyrol on 28 February 2026. The incident occurred during a period of elevated avalanche danger across the Eastern Alps and forms part of the broader February 2026 avalanche cycle. The post Two Ski Mountaineers Found Dead After Avalanche on Tamlspitze, Tyrol appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: Repeated avalanche fatalities across the Alps in late February 2026 continue to underline the structural instability affecting multiple regions this winter. The latest tragedy occurred on Tamlspitze , in the municipality of Tux , within the Austrian state of Tyrol , where two ski mountaineers aged 44 and 47 were found dead beneath avalanche debris after failing to return from a summit tour. This incident forms part of the broader February 2026 Avalanche Cycle Across the Alps , which has seen fatalities and repeated Level 4 warnings across France, Italy, Austria and Switzerland. What Happened on Tamlspitze According to Austrian local media reports and police information, the two men set out on 28 February for what was intended to be a standard ski mountaineering ascent of Tamlspitze in the Tux Valley. When they did not return, they were reported missing later that day. Alpine Rescue teams initiated search operations immediately. However, persistent avalanche danger in the region forced

articles.title: Avalanche Fatality at Nakiska Ski Area
articles.description: The Nakiska avalanche in Kananaskis Country, Alberta, left a 15-year-old skier dead. Authorities confirmed two people were caught in the slide. The post Avalanche Fatality at Nakiska Ski Area appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: A 15-year-old skier has died after being buried in an avalanche near the Nakiska Ski Area in Kananaskis Country, Alberta on Friday, February 28, 2026 . Emergency responders were called to the area after reports that two people were caught in the avalanche . One skier escaped and was uninjured, while the other — the 15-year-old — was found unresponsive and later died at Alberta Children’s Hospital. Officials say the incident occurred near the Nakiska ski hill — west of Calgary — and authorities are continuing their investigation into the exact circumstances. Avalanche Danger in Kananaskis Country Avalanche Canada, the national public avalanche safety organization responsible for forecasting avalanche conditions across western Canada, provides ongoing danger ratings for Kananaskis Country and other mountainous regions. As of recent regional forecasts: Avalanche danger in parts of Kananaskis Country has been rated as Considerable (Level 3) or Moderate (Level 2) depending on elevation and

articles.title: Avalanche Hits Two Pistes at La Flégère, Chamonix — Skiers Rescued, No Fatalities
articles.description: Chamonix, France – 24 February 2026 An avalanche struck two marked ski runs in the La Flégère sector of the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc ski area on Tuesday, briefly burying three skiers before they were rescued safely. The slide crossed sections of the red Crochues piste and the black Floria run around midday. According to regional newspaper Le Dauphiné Libéré, the avalanche debris spread across approximately 100 … Continue reading "Avalanche Hits Two Pistes at La Flégère, Chamonix — Skiers Rescued, No
articles.content: Chamonix, France – 24 February 2026 An avalanche struck two marked ski runs in the La Flégère sector of the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc ski area on Tuesday, briefly burying three skiers before they were rescued safely. The slide crossed sections of the red Crochues piste and the black Floria run around midday. According to regional newspaper Le Dauphiné Libéré , the avalanche debris spread across approximately 100 metres of terrain within the ski domain. What Happened at La Flégère? The avalanche released above the pistes in the Floria sector of La Flégère, one of the main lift-accessed areas in Chamonix. Three skiers were partially buried — reportedly up to chest height — but were quickly located and freed by piste patrol and rescue teams. None are believed to have suffered life-threatening injuries. Given the scale of the slide, a full rescue operation was launched as a precaution. Ongoing Avalanche Activity Across the Alps This incident in Chamonix comes amid a broader avalanche cycle affec
